name: Rosemary

book_claims.claim: Contains rosmarinic acid and carnosic acid with potential antioxidant, CNS-protective and liver-protective properties
book_claims.mechanism: Rosmarinic acid and carnosic acid are potent antioxidant compounds with neuroprotective and hepatoprotective activity

supplementary_research.finding: Comprehensive review found carnosic acid and carnosol from rosemary demonstrate neuroprotective effects in models of Alzheimer's and Parkinson's disease through Nrf2 pathway activation and reduction of oxidative stress. Supports the book's CNS-protective claims.

Rosemary aroma exposure improved cognitive performance (speed and accuracy) in healthy adults. Plasma levels of 1,8-cineole (a rosemary volatile) correlated with cognitive performance, suggesting absorption through inhalation.

Rosemary extract significantly reduces lipid oxidation in cooked meats, lowering formation of malondialdehyde and heterocyclic amines -- carcinogenic compounds formed during high-heat cooking. Practical food safety application beyond supplementation.

Moderate evidence for cognitive enhancement and neuroprotection. Carnosic acid identified as primary active compound with antioxidant activity that increases under oxidative stress conditions (pro-electrophilic mechanism).

culinary_pairings.source: Classic pairing for roasted vegetables, meats, and focaccia
Infused rosemary oil for bread dipping and roasting
Brightens rosemary's resinous quality in marinades and dressings
Essential in Tuscan white bean dishes (fagioli all'uccelletto)
Roasted rosemary potatoes are a universal pairing
Rosemary-walnut combinations in breads, pestos, and salads
Both Lamiaceae herbs; combined in Italian roasts and compound butters

seasonality: Evergreen perennial available year-round. Grows easily in pots or garden beds in temperate climates. Fresh rosemary is always preferable to dried for flavour, though dried retains significant active compounds.

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Pro-electrophilic: becomes more potent antioxidant under oxidative stress. Activates Nrf2 pathway. Lipophilic -- pair with fats for absorption.
Water-soluble polyphenol well-extracted in teas and infusions. Anti-inflammatory via COX-2 inhibition. Moderate oral bioavailability.

nutrient_highlights.amount_per_100g: Major volatile compound in essential oil
nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Absorbed through both inhalation and ingestion. Linked to cognitive performance improvements in human trials.
Oxidation product of carnosic acid with independent anticancer and anti-inflammatory activity.

synergies.description: Both Lamiaceae family herbs rich in hydroxycinnamic acids; combined provide broader antioxidant coverage
Fat carrier enhances absorption of lipophilic carnosic acid
